commit.message:
Smart HTML cleaning: extract content elements instead of truncating raw HTML

Previous approach sent first 20K chars of raw HTML to Claude, which was mostly
CSS/table markup with only 27-52% of actual text content. Now extracts headings,
paragraphs, lists, and links into clean markdown-like text. All 10 newsletters
now get 100%+ content coverage in 6-25K chars (vs 42-108K raw HTML).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Fix(checkin): early expiry on checkout only

Move early checkout expiry logic from keymaster
unlock handler to async_checkout service. Per
FR-022/FR-023, lock code expiry should only be
shortened on manual checkout, not on every
keymaster unlock while checked_in.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Signed-off-by: Andrew Grimberg <tykeal@bardicgrove.org>

commit.message:
Fix rate limiting: trim HTML to 20K chars, increase delay to 15s between calls

Anthropic org limit is 30K input tokens/min. Previous settings (50K char HTML, 2s delay)
were blowing through the limit immediately. Now sends smaller prompts with adequate spacing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: accessibility and performance improvements (#9, #10)

- Extract inline CSS to external files (index.css, agenda.css, labs.css)
  for browser caching and reduced render-blocking
- Add <meta name=description> to all three pages for SEO
- Add <link rel=icon> to all pages to prevent 404 on favicon
- Wrap page content in <main> semantic landmark on all pages
- Add aria-label to emoji nav links in index.html
- Add scope=col to all <th> elements in agenda.html and labs.html
- Add rel=noopener noreferrer to all target=_blank links in labs.html

Closes #10

commit.message:
Simplify system configuration and service pricing

- Remove features field from tier_system_configurations table
  * Created migration script to drop features column

  * Removed features UI from system config panel

- Fix system config panel save button labels
  * Changed from 'Save Good/Better/Best' to just 'Save'

commit.message:
Add no-console ESLint rule (#583)

Adds `no-console` rule to prevent accidental debugging statements in
production code.

## Changes

- Added `"no-console": "error"` to `.eslintrc.json` rules section

The rule enforces that console methods (log, warn, error, etc.) cannot
be used in linted source files. Build scripts and test configurations
remain unaffected as they fall outside the linting scope.

```json
"rules": {
  "react/react-in-jsx-scope": "off",
  "@typescript-eslint/consistent-type-imports": "error",
  "no-console": "error"
}
```

commit.message:
Move UI concerns out of library

- Remove FORMAT_OPTIONS, memory helpers (getDefaultMemoryLimitMB,
  getDeviceMaxMemoryMB, MEMORY_STEP, MEMORY_MIN_MB) — these are
  consumer-side UI configuration
- Remove extractLabel and toGeoJSON from ExtentData — presentation
  logic belongs in the caller
- ExtentData.fetchExtents now returns raw nested data:
  rgExtents is { filename: { rg_N: bbox } } instead of flattened
- MetadataProvider.getParquetUrl is now identity (no .pmtiles/.mosaic.json
  assumption) — consumers subclass for custom URL schemes
- DefaultMetadataProvider.getMetaJsonUrl derives from getParquetUrl()
- Add WIP notice to README

commit.message:
Add stratum reconcile command; atomic uploads with 429 backoff

- Switch publish_to_hub from per-file upload_file to single
  upload_folder commit (atomic — all files land or none do)
- Add _retry_on_429 with exponential backoff (10s initial, 5 retries)

and per-pixel surface normal prediction.

position.

tooltip is already gone. The `mouseenter` handler is simplified, and
tooltips are properly cleaned up on navigation and frame loads.

showing tooltips.

commit.message:
Address PR #1856 review feedback: fix fail-open, tree-ish parsing, pathspec-from-file

Fix three issues identified in code review:

1. HIGH: AddStagedFilesToModifiedPaths now returns failure when
   git diff --cached exits non-zero, instead of silently succeeding
   with addedCount=0 (which re-opened the corruption this PR fixes).

2. MEDIUM: GetRestorePathspec now strips the tree-ish argument for
   checkout commands (e.g. HEAD in 'checkout HEAD -- file'), and
   skips --source/-s values for restore commands, preventing
   accidental pathspec scope widening.

3. MEDIUM: --pathspec-from-file and --pathspec-file-nul are now
   forwarded through the IPC protocol to the mount process, which
   passes them directly to git diff --cached. The hook never reads
   the file itself -- git handles all pathspec parsing on both sides.
   Stdin (-) is unsupported in hook context and fails closed.

Refactors parsing logic into UnstageCommandParser (public static class
in a separate file) so it can be linked into GVFS.UnitTests without
pulling in the full GVFS.Hooks Exe assembly.

DiffCachedNameStatus now accepts optional pathspecFromFile and
pathspecFileNul parameters.

IPC message body format extended:
  null/empty        -> all staged files
  path1\0path2      -> inline pathspecs (unchanged)
  \nF\n<filepath>   -> --pathspec-from-file
  \nFZ\n<filepath>  -> --pathspec-from-file + --pathspec-file-nul

Adds 28 unit tests covering IsUnstageOperation and GetRestorePathspec.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Fix DINOv3 register token ordering in pipeline and visualization

DINOv3 token sequence is [CLS, reg1..reg4, patch1..patchN], not
[CLS, patch1..patchN, reg1..reg4] as previously assumed.

Pipeline: slice from index 5 onward to get only spatial patches.
Visualization: auto-detect and strip leading register tokens in
existing data (first 4 have ~1.6× higher norm than spatial patches).

commit.message:
Address PR feedback: proper exit codes and error classification for mount lock

- Add TryAcquireLock(out Exception) to FileBasedLock so callers can
  pattern-match on exception type to distinguish lock contention
  (IOException/sharing violation) from permission or I/O errors.
- Replace bare return with FailMountAndExit() when lock acquisition
  fails, so the process exits with a non-zero exit code.
- Add ReturnCode.MountAlreadyRunning (8) for the lock contention case,
  keeping GenericError (3) for unexpected lock failures.
- Add FailMountAndExit(ReturnCode, ...) overload to support per-call
  exit codes while preserving existing callers.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Move config.yaml out of version control

Bundle a default config as loki/config.default.yaml inside the package.
On `loki setup`, copy it to the project root only if config.yaml does not
already exist, so user edits are never overwritten. The existing config
review flow (display + confirm) is unchanged regardless of whether the
file was just seeded from defaults or already present.

- Add loki/config.default.yaml (bundled defaults, committed)
- Remove config.yaml from git tracking (git rm --cached); add to .gitignore
- setup(): copy bundled default before load_config() when file is absent
- load_config(): improve missing-file error to point users to `loki setup`
- tests: add copy-on-missing and no-overwrite-on-existing test cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Use hardlinks for npy migration with copy fallback

Hardlinks avoid doubling ~280 GB of disk usage when source and destination
are on the same filesystem. Falls back to shutil.copy2() for cross-filesystem
migrations.

Tested: 69,995 FFHQ images migrated in ~12 min (vs ~30 hr for full copy).

commit.message:
Add migrate command for converting prx-tg per-modality format to stratum per-image format

Adds 'stratum migrate' subcommand that reads the old approved_image_dataset.jsonl
and per-modality npy directories (dinov3/, t5_hidden/, etc.) and creates stratum
per-image directories with properly renamed artifacts.

Features:
- Copies npy files with correct naming (dinov3/{id}.npy -> {id}/dinov3_cls.npy, etc.)
- Converts JSONL t5_attention_mask lists to t5_mask.npy (uint8)
- Extracts captions to caption.txt and metadata to metadata.json
- --dry-run mode for preview without writing
- Progress reporting with throughput stats
- Skips already-migrated images (idempotent)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
